_comment: J1.2 entailment calibration set. Hand-authored (claim, span, context, gold_verdict) fixtures with human ground-truth GOLD labels. Fields: pair_id, claim_type, category, adversarial_type (null unless category=adversarial), claim{claim_id,claim_text}, span{source_id,quote,section} (span may be null), context, source_metadata, gold_verdict in {supported,partial,unsupported,contradicted,not_evaluable}, note. Quotes are verbatim from research/benchmarks/held-out/corpus. This is a category-complete SEED slice (~debug tier). See research/benchmarks/CALIBRATION.md for the PENDING expansion to >=150-250 stratified pairs before decision-tier gating.

claim.claim_text: Torque v2.0.0 was released on 2026-01-15.

span.quote: Torque v2.0.0 reached general availability on 2026-01-15.

note: Span directly and fully asserts the release date.

claim.claim_text: Torque is a Rust stream-processing engine.

span.quote: It is a Rust stream-processing engine maintained by Torque Labs and released under the Apache-2.0 license.

note: Span states the claim verbatim.

claim.claim_text: Torque v2.0.0 is licensed under Apache-2.0.

span.quote: Torque v2.0.0 is licensed under Apache-2.0.

note: Exact verbatim support.

claim.claim_text: Torque Labs raised an $18M Series A.

span.quote: Torque Labs raised an $18M Series A on 2026-02-20.

note: Span fully supports the amount and round.

claim.claim_text: Torque Labs' Series A was led by Northwind Ventures.

span.quote: The round was led by Northwind Ventures.

note: Direct support.

claim.claim_text: TQ-SA-2026-001 is a deserialization remote-code-execution vulnerability.

span.quote: TQ-SA-2026-001 discloses a deserialization remote-code-execution (RCE) vulnerability in Torque, published 2026-04-02.

note: Span directly asserts the vulnerability class.

claim.claim_text: The TQ-SA-2026-001 vulnerability is fixed in Torque v2.1.1.

span.quote: The vulnerability is fixed in v2.1.1; all users must upgrade immediately.

note: Span asserts the fix version.

claim.claim_text: Only Torque Labs employees may merge changes to Torque.

span.quote: External contributors may submit PRs but cannot approve or merge changes.

note: Span supports that merge rights are restricted to Torque Labs.

claim.claim_text: In the vendor benchmark, Torque sustains 1.2M events/sec on a single 8-core node.

span.quote: Torque sustains 1.2M events/sec on a single 8-core node in the vendor test.

note: Verbatim support of the vendor figure, correctly attributed to the vendor test.

claim.claim_text: The TQ-SA-2026-001 vulnerability has a CVSS base score of 9.1.

span.quote: The vulnerability is a deserialization RCE with CVSS base score 9.1 (critical).

note: Direct support of the score.

claim.claim_text: Torque Labs is profitable.

span.quote: Torque Labs raised an $18M Series A on 2026-02-20.

note: Raising a round says nothing about profitability; span does not bear on the claim.

claim.claim_text: Torque sustains 1.2M events/sec on a 4-core node.

span.quote: Torque sustains 1.2M events/sec on a single 8-core node in the vendor test.

note: Span is about an 8-core node, not 4-core; the claim's hardware is not supported.

claim.claim_text: Torque has a formal foundation-based governance model.

span.quote: All maintainer and merge rights are held exclusively by Torque Labs employees.

note: Span describes single-vendor control, not a foundation model.

claim.claim_text: Torque v1.9 is affected by TQ-SA-2026-001.

span.quote: Affected versions: 2.0.0 through 2.1.0 inclusive.

note: v1.9 is outside the stated affected range; span does not support the claim.

claim.claim_text: Torque v2.1.0 added a Redis sink connector.

span.quote: v2.1.0 adds incremental checkpointing and a new Kafka source connector.

note: Span lists checkpointing and Kafka connector, not a Redis sink.

claim.claim_text: The Torque repository has 30,000 GitHub stars.

span.quote: The Torque repository has 14,200 stars and 187 contributors.

note: Span reports 14,200 stars, not 30,000.

claim.claim_text: The default processing guarantee in Torque v2.0.0 is exactly_once.

span.quote: Exactly-once is enabled by setting `processing.guarantee = exactly_once` in the `torque.toml` configuration file; the default remains `at_least_once`.

note: Span states the default is at_least_once, incompatible with the claim.

claim.claim_text: Torque's 2.x releases are licensed under the Business Source License 1.1.

span.quote: Existing 2.x releases remain Apache-2.0; the change is not retroactive.

note: Span asserts 2.x remains Apache-2.0, contradicting the BSL claim.

claim.claim_text: Torque provides true exactly-once delivery across the Kafka sink in v2.1.0.

span.quote: Claims true exactly-once is not achievable across the Kafka sink in v2.1.0.

note: Span directly denies exactly-once across the Kafka sink.

claim.claim_text: The windowed-aggregation memory leak was already fixed in Torque v2.1.0.

span.quote: A memory leak in windowed aggregation under sustained high load is a KNOWN UNRESOLVED issue in v2.1.0, tracked in GitHub issue #812.

note: Span says the leak is unresolved in v2.1.0, contradicting 'already fixed'.

claim.claim_text: External contributors can approve and merge Torque changes.

span.quote: External contributors may submit PRs but cannot approve or merge changes.

note: Span explicitly denies external merge rights.

claim.claim_text: Torque v2.0.0 was released on 2026-03-10.

span.quote: Torque v2.0.0 reached general availability on 2026-01-15.

note: Span gives a different, incompatible release date.

claim.claim_text: Torque v2.0.0 was released on 2026-01-15 and it is written in Rust.

span.quote: Torque v2.0.0 reached general availability on 2026-01-15.

note: Compound claim (two independent clauses); must be decomposed before judging.

claim.claim_text: The RCE is critical and all users must upgrade because the fix ships in v2.1.1.

span.quote: The vulnerability is fixed in v2.1.1; all users must upgrade immediately.

note: Compound: recommendation ('must') plus a causal clause ('because').

claim.claim_text: Torque Labs' Series A was $18M, and it was led by Northwind Ventures.

span.quote: Torque Labs raised an $18M Series A on 2026-02-20.

note: Compound: two independent predicates.

claim.claim_text: Torque Labs raised an $18M Series A.

note: No exact evidence span to evaluate; judge returns not_evaluable without a provider.

claim.claim_text: Torque governance is single-vendor.

note: Empty/whitespace span carries no verbatim quote to judge.

claim.claim_text: Torque's throughput is 1.2M events/sec, and its memory footprint is 2GB.

span.quote: Torque sustains 1.2M events/sec on a single 8-core node in the vendor test.

note: Compound: throughput predicate plus an unrelated memory predicate.

claim.claim_text: Torque Labs raised a $30M Series B.

span.quote: Torque Labs raised an $18M Series A on 2026-02-20.

note: Quote is genuine but about an $18M Series A, not a $30M Series B.

claim.claim_text: Torque v2.1.0 introduced exactly-once processing semantics.

span.quote: v2.0.0 introduces exactly-once processing semantics for windowed aggregations.

note: Real quote, but it attributes exactly-once to v2.0.0, not v2.1.0.

claim.claim_text: The deserialization RCE is fixed in v2.1.0.

span.quote: The vulnerability is fixed in v2.1.1; all users must upgrade immediately.

note: Genuine quote says v2.1.1, not v2.1.0.

claim.claim_text: Torque is a community-neutral project.

span.quote: Governance is therefore single-vendor controlled, not community-neutral.

note: The verbatim quote asserts the opposite of the claim.

claim.claim_text: The Torque repository has 187 stars.

span.quote: The Torque repository has 14,200 stars and 187 contributors.

note: 187 is the contributor count, not the star count; correct quote, wrong field.

claim.claim_text: PR #905 has already been merged.

span.quote: The proposal is under discussion and not yet merged.

note: Real quote states it is not yet merged.

claim.claim_text: Torque v2.1.0 added incremental checkpointing and a Kafka source connector.

span.quote: v2.1.0 adds incremental checkpointing

note: Truncated quote supports only checkpointing, omitting the Kafka connector half of the claim.

claim.claim_text: The RCE is a critical deserialization flaw affecting versions 2.0.0 through 2.1.0.

span.quote: The vulnerability is a deserialization RCE with CVSS base score 9.1 (critical).

note: Quote supports the criticality/class but not the affected-version range in the claim.

claim.claim_text: Torque Labs raised an $18M Series A led by Northwind Ventures.

span.quote: Torque Labs raised an $18M Series A on 2026-02-20.

note: Quote supports the amount but not the lead-investor element of the claim.

claim.claim_text: The memory leak grows the heap unbounded until the worker is OOM-killed after ~40 minutes.

span.quote: Heap grows unbounded

note: Truncated quote supports unbounded heap growth but omits the OOM-kill timing.

claim.claim_text: Torque sustains 1.2M events/sec on an 8-core node running a 5-second tumbling-window aggregation.

span.quote: Torque sustains 1.2M events/sec on a single 8-core node in the vendor test.

note: Quote supports throughput and hardware but omits the workload described in the claim.

claim.claim_text: OOM-kills of the worker are what cause the memory leak in windowed aggregation.

span.quote: Heap grows unbounded until the worker is OOM-killed after roughly 40 minutes.

note: Span has the leak causing OOM-kills; the claim reverses the direction.

claim.claim_text: Enabling exactly-once is what sets `processing.guarantee = exactly_once` in torque.toml.

span.quote: Exactly-once is enabled by setting `processing.guarantee = exactly_once` in the `torque.toml` configuration file

note: Span: setting the config enables exactly-once; the claim inverts cause and effect.

claim.claim_text: The Series A funding was raised in order to fund work that had already shipped v3.0.

span.quote: Proceeds fund the v3.0 distributed engine and a managed cloud offering.

note: Span: proceeds will fund future v3.0 work; the claim reverses the temporal/causal order.

claim.claim_text: The security advisory was published because users upgraded to v2.1.1.

span.quote: The vulnerability is fixed in v2.1.1; all users must upgrade immediately.

note: Span: upgrade follows the advisory/fix; claim reverses the causal order.

claim.claim_text: Torque v2.0.0 provides exactly-once processing semantics.

span.quote: v2.0.0 introduces exactly-once processing semantics for windowed aggregations.

note: Claim drops the 'for windowed aggregations' scope, overgeneralising the span.

claim.claim_text: Torque sustains 1.2M events/sec.

span.quote: Torque sustains 1.2M events/sec on a single 8-core node in the vendor test.

note: Claim omits the single-8-core-node and vendor-test qualifiers.

claim.claim_text: The Torque memory leak reproduces on v2.1.0.

span.quote: The leak reproduces on v2.1.0 with windows held open under sustained load.

note: Claim omits the 'windows held open under sustained load' precondition.

claim.claim_text: Torque is moving to the Business Source License 1.1.

span.quote: PR #905 proposes moving Torque from Apache-2.0 to the Business Source License (BSL) 1.1 starting with v3.0.

note: Claim drops that it is only a proposal and only from v3.0; span is conditional.

claim.claim_text: All Torque users must upgrade immediately.

span.quote: The vulnerability is fixed in v2.1.1; all users must upgrade immediately.

note: Claim omits the target version (v2.1.1); span ties the upgrade to that fix.

claim.claim_text: As of 2026-05-01, v2.1.0 is the latest stable Torque release.

span.quote: v2.1.0 is the latest stable release as of 2026-03-10.

note: Span establishes 'latest' only as of 2026-03-10; the claim's later date is not supported.

claim.claim_text: Issue #812 was already resolved as of 2026-03-12.

span.quote: Status in snapshot 01: OPEN / unresolved; a fix is planned for the next patch.

note: As of the snapshot date the issue is open; the claim's temporal state is contradicted.

claim.claim_text: Torque had already shipped its exactly-once semantics by 2025-06-01.

span.quote: Torque v2.0.0 reached general availability on 2026-01-15.

note: GA was 2026-01-15; a 2025 date is outside the supported temporal scope.

claim.claim_text: The Series A round closed in 2025.

span.quote: Torque Labs raised an $18M Series A on 2026-02-20.

note: Span dates the round to 2026-02-20; the 2025 claim is contradicted.

claim.claim_text: As of 2026-04-15, the deserialization RCE remains unpatched.

span.quote: The vulnerability is fixed in v2.1.1; all users must upgrade immediately.

note: A fix exists as of the 2026-04-02 advisory; 'remains unpatched' later is contradicted.

claim.claim_text: Torque delivers true exactly-once semantics.

span.quote: A community discussion thread disputing Torque's exactly-once marketing.

note: Span merely says a thread discusses exactly-once; it does not assert the claim.

claim.claim_text: Torque outperforms every competitor on throughput.

span.quote: The report is authored and run by Torque Labs (vendor self-benchmark).

note: Span notes authorship; it does not support a competitor comparison.

claim.claim_text: Torque governance risk is low.

span.quote: An investment analyst memo evaluating Torque as an opportunity.

note: Span describes what the memo is; it does not itself assert the risk level.

claim.claim_text: The memory leak has been root-caused to a specific allocation site.

span.quote: Issue #812 reports a memory leak in windowed aggregation under sustained high load on Torque v2.1.0.

note: Span reports the leak exists; it does not support a root-cause allocation site.

claim.claim_text: Torque has adopted a dual-licensing model.

span.quote: PR #905 proposes relicensing Torque for the v3.0 line.

note: Span mentions a relicensing proposal; it does not support 'dual-licensing adopted'.

claim.claim_text: Torque v2.0.0 was released on 2026-01-15.

span.quote: ingested_at: '2026-01-16'

note: The quote is the note's ingest date from frontmatter, not evidence of the release date.

claim.claim_text: The 1.2M events/sec benchmark is a primary, high-strength source.

span.quote: evidence_strength: maintainer-commentary

note: A frontmatter tag is metadata about the note, not substantive support for the claim.

claim.claim_text: Torque is governed by Torque Labs.

span.quote: organization: Torque Labs

note: The 'organization' frontmatter names who published the note, not a governance finding.

claim.claim_text: The advisory is an official Torque Labs document.

span.quote: type: source-summary

note: The 'type' frontmatter is note metadata, not evidence about the advisory's authority.

claim.claim_text: StreamWeekly independently corroborates Torque's 1.2M events/sec throughput.

span.quote: States that Torque sustains 1.2M events/sec on an 8-core node.

note: StreamWeekly quotes the same vendor figure; it does not independently corroborate it.

claim.claim_text: DataEng Digest provides an independent throughput measurement for Torque.

span.quote: Cites 1.2M events/sec sustained throughput for Torque on an 8-core node.

note: The figure traces to the same vendor benchmark; no independent measurement is provided.

claim.claim_text: Two independent sources confirm Torque's 1.2M events/sec figure.

span.quote: The 1.2M figure is quoted directly from the Torque Labs vendor benchmark

note: The span states the figure is quoted from the single vendor benchmark, denying independence.

claim.claim_text: An independent analyst confirms Torque is community-driven.

span.quote: Describes Torque as a community-driven, vendor-neutral open-source project.

note: An unverified model-generated lead is not independent corroboration of the claim.
